story_title: Ensure Security Compliance for Collaboration Features
description: Review and ensure that the new collaboration features comply with security and data protection regulations.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the new collaboration features are developed WHEN a compliance audit is conducted THEN all features meet security and data protection standards.

description: Develop a real-time co-editing feature that allows multiple users to edit documents simultaneously, addressing the critical need identified by enterprise customers.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a document is open for editing WHEN multiple users access the document THEN all users can edit simultaneously without locking.

description: Redesign the document locking mechanism to eliminate user frustration caused by the current model, allowing for smoother collaboration.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a document is being edited WHEN another user attempts to access it THEN they are notified of the current editing status without being locked out.

description: Introduce presence indicators to show who is currently viewing or editing a document, enhancing collaboration efficiency.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN multiple users are accessing a document WHEN they are viewing or editing THEN their presence is indicated by avatars in the document header.

description: Implement monitoring for concurrent edit attempts that are blocked to ensure the new collaboration features are functioning as intended.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the new collaboration features are live WHEN concurrent edit attempts occur THEN the number of blocked attempts is tracked and reported.

description: Create comprehensive documentation for the new real-time collaboration features to assist users and support teams.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the new collaboration features are launched WHEN users access the documentation THEN they can find clear instructions and support information.

description: Establish a conflict resolution strategy for concurrent edits to prevent data loss and ensure user changes are preserved.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN two users are editing the same document WHEN they make conflicting changes THEN the system resolves the conflict according to the chosen strategy without data loss.

description: Improve the mobile editing experience to support real-time collaboration and ensure it is on par with desktop functionality.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user is editing on mobile WHEN they access a document THEN they can collaborate in real-time with other users without issues.

story_title: Collect User Feedback on Collaboration Features
description: Establish a mechanism to gather user feedback specifically on the new collaboration features to ensure they meet user needs.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN new collaboration features are launched WHEN users interact with them THEN feedback is collected through surveys and analytics.

story_title: Conduct A/B Testing on Collaboration Features
description: Perform A/B testing on different implementations of the real-time collaboration features to determine which version yields higher user engagement.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN different versions of collaboration features are available WHEN users interact with them THEN engagement metrics are collected and analyzed.

story_title: Competitor launched real-time co-editing — we need parity
description: Competitor X (Notion-adjacent) launched live co-editing on 2025-10-15 with a heavy marketing push directly targeting our enterprise segment. Sales has fielded 14 inbound inquiries in two weeks asking when CollabDocs will support simultaneous editing. Without parity we risk losing competitive positioning in the enterprise document space. This ticket tracks the initiative to deliver real-time co-editing at feature parity or better.

Users have no visibility into who else is currently viewing or editing a document. This causes duplicate work (two people editing the same section simultaneously then manually merging) and communication overhead. Requested feature: real-time presence avatars in the document header showing active viewers and editors, with cursor position indicators per user. This is a prerequisite for real-time co-editing and has standalone value for read-only collaboration workflows.
